title: The nature of neural object representations during dynamic occlusion
description: This magnetoencephalography (MEG) dataset investigates neural object representations during dynamic visual occlusion. Participants viewed objects that either disappeared or became occluded during MEG recording, with concurrent eye-tracking. The dataset includes raw MEG data, behavioral responses, eye-tracking recordings, and preprocessed derivatives formatted for multivariate pattern analysis, enabling investigation of how the brain maintains object representations when visual input is interrupted.
methods_description: MEG data were collected while participants viewed objects undergoing dynamic occlusion or disappearance. Behavioral responses and eye-tracking data (pupil size, eye position) were recorded simultaneously during the MEG session across multiple runs. Data were preprocessed and epoched relative to stimulus onset and position changes using CoSMoMVPA formatting.
